title: Bulk add Sub items into a long list of items
excerpt: I have a contact list of schools that I am to work with and have a sub item list set as a master that sits within the item (Contact) and is set up ready to use. If I import the contact list of schools from excel, how wou…

posts.author: Peteskis (Peter)
posts.cookedHtml: I have a contact list of schools that I am to work with and have a sub item list set as a master that sits within the item (Contact) and is set up ready to use. If I import the contact list of schools from excel, how would I add the sub items to all of the contacts in one go? Is that possible?. I am talking a list of 300 schools, so adding the sub items to each manually isn’t practical. Is there a straight forward way of adding a list of sub items to a new item created, especially when the list of new items are 300 contacts long?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Instead of using subitems for contacts, what about having another board with the contacts and then use a connected column to link the two?

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: i-jaglan (Sagar Jaglan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Peter! I ran into this exact problem and ended up building
an app for it - SubItems Pro in the marketplace.

It lets you:

  • Select multiple items → bulk create subitems
  • Clone subitems from one item to many others
  • Optionally inherit status/person/date from parent

Happy to answer any questions!

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @Peteskis At the moment, monday.com doesn’t support bulk-adding a preset list of subitems to multiple items during or after an Excel import. Subitems can’t be imported at scale using the native importer.

The most practical options are:

Use an automation so that when an item is created, the required subitems are automatically added. This works well for newly imported items and going forward.

Use a marketplace import app that supports importing subitems from Excel, since this isn’t available natively yet.

Duplicate an item that already contains the full subitem structure if you only need to create a smaller number manually.

Dr. Tanvi Sachar
Monday Certified Partner, Tuesday Wizard

posts.author: MsquareAutomation (Msquare Automation Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Peteskis
Yes — this is absolutely possible using Make Automation

Since monday.com doesn’t provide a native way to bulk attach the same subitem template to hundreds of items, you can automate it externally.

How it would work

A Make scenario can:

  • Retrieve all newly imported contact items (your 300 schools)

  • For each item, automatically create the predefined subitems

  • Populate each subitem with the same structure, names, and default values from your “master” list

You only need to define the subitem template once inside the scenario, and it will replicate it across all parent items in one run.
